definition: to clear someone of blame or suspicion; to show to be right or justified
song_title: Look What You Made Me Do
sentence: Everything she'd done was, in her mind, an attempt to __________ herself — to prove the story the world told about her was wrong.

definition: famous or well known, typically for some bad quality or deed
song_title: Look What You Made Me Do
sentence: She leaned into being __________, deciding that if they were going to tell her story, she'd write the ending herself.

definition: to speak about someone in a spitefully critical manner; evil in nature
song_title: This Is Why We Can't Have Nice Things
sentence: They had spent years trying to __________ her, twisting every word she said into evidence for the case against her.

definition: showing open resistance or bold disobedience
song_title: ...Ready for It?
sentence: She arrived at the show looking __________ — daring anyone in the crowd or the press box to underestimate her again.

definition: deceitful; saying one thing while meaning or doing another
song_title: End Game
sentence: She had trusted him completely, which made discovering how __________ he'd been all along hit twice as hard.

definition: guilty of betrayal; dangerous and unpredictable
song_title: Getaway Car
sentence: She knew the road they were on was __________ — beautiful and reckless, leading somewhere neither of them had planned for.

definition: wicked or criminal in nature
song_title: I Did Something Bad
sentence: The tabloids painted her as something __________, dangerous and calculating — a villain in a story they'd written without her input.

definition: having or showing skill in achieving one's ends by deceit or evasion
song_title: End Game
sentence: He mistook her for reckless when she was actually __________, already ten moves ahead of everyone in the room.

definition: to make an attack in return for a similar attack
song_title: Look What You Made Me Do
sentence: Rather than apologize and retreat, she chose to __________ — using her music as the weapon they'd always accused her of having.

definition: a mark of disgrace associated with a particular circumstance or quality
song_title: This Is Why We Can't Have Nice Things
sentence: She had spent years fighting the __________ attached to her name, the assumption that she was always the problem.

definition: seeking to undermine the power of an established system or institution
song_title: Look What You Made Me Do
sentence: The video was deliberately __________ — dismantling the very image people had constructed of her while they watched.

definition: having or showing a strong desire to harm someone who has harmed you
song_title: Bad Blood
sentence: Her critics called her __________, but she saw it differently: she was simply someone who refused to absorb cruelty in silence.

definition: to speak or write about in an abusively disparaging manner
song_title: This Is Why We Can't Have Nice Things
sentence: The media would __________ her no matter what she did, so she decided to give them something worth writing about.

definition: the state of being well known for some bad quality or deed
song_title: Look What You Made Me Do
sentence: She had earned a kind of __________ she hadn't asked for, and she was determined to alchemize it into power.

definition: bold and without shame; made of brass
song_title: ...Ready for It?
sentence: She was __________ in a way the critics hadn't expected — not apologetic, not defensive, but entirely, dangerously unafraid.

definition: to exclude from a society or group
song_title: Delicate
sentence: She feared being __________ by people who only knew the version of her that existed in headlines.

definition: to express sharp disapproval or criticism of someone because of their behavior
song_title: This Is Why We Can't Have Nice Things
sentence: She delivered a sharp public __________ to every fake friend who had smiled at her while talking behind her back.

definition: causing annoyance, anger, or another strong reaction; deliberately stimulating
song_title: I Did Something Bad
sentence: Her performance was deliberately __________, designed to unsettle the people who had grown comfortable dismissing her.

definition: a person who acts obsequiously toward someone powerful for personal gain
song_title: This Is Why We Can't Have Nice Things
sentence: She could always tell a __________ from a real friend — one showed up only when the spotlight was on.

definition: to express severe disapproval of someone, especially in a formal statement
song_title: Look What You Made Me Do
sentence: The critics chose to publicly __________ her at every turn, which only gave her more material to work with.
